pa_legislature.split_control: Democrats control House by 1 seat; Republicans control Senate
key_notes: February-March 2026: Legislative recess for Governor's FY 2026-27 budget hearings (currently ongoing as of March 15, 2026)
2025 budget was 135 days late (signed November 12, 2025) — transit funding was core unresolved issue
All House seats and even-numbered Senate seats up for election November 2026
Democrats control House by ONE seat; Republicans control Senate
May 19, 2026: Primary elections (candidate petitions due; deadline passed March 11, 2026)

house_session_dates.notes: Next session: House reconvenes Monday March 23, 2026

senate_session_dates.notes: No Sept/Oct/Nov dates yet published for Senate

active_bills.title: Public Transportation Trust Fund Transfers and Increases
active_bills.short_description: Governor Shapiro's plan to fund transit in all 67 counties. Creates Road and Bridge Project Fund, Transportation Funding Advisory Commission, authorizes CFA bonds for road/bridge projects.
active_bills.status: Passed House 107-96 on June 17, 2025. Referred to Senate Transportation Committee June 18, 2025. STALLED in Senate.
active_bills.pittsburgh_impact: Would provide $292M in new recurring transit funding growing to $1.5B over 5 years, directly benefiting PRT.
active_bills.notes: Senate Republican leadership blocked; PRT used $106.7M in capital funds to cover operating deficit instead.

active_bills.title: Transit for All PA legislative package — House companion
active_bills.short_description: Would raise dedicated, sufficient revenue for PA transit systems statewide including PRT. Part of the Transit for All PA coalition package.
active_bills.status: Introduced; House side of bipartisan transit funding package
active_bills.pittsburgh_impact: PRT funding stabilization; would address structural $100M+ annual deficit

active_bills.title: Transit for All PA legislative package — House companion (2)
active_bills.short_description: Part of the five-bill Transit for All PA House package.
active_bills.status: Introduced; passed House as part of five-bill package; Senate stalled

active_bills.title: Transit for All PA — Senate companion
active_bills.short_description: Senate version of dedicated revenue for PA transit systems.
active_bills.status: Introduced; Senate Republican leadership has not scheduled for floor vote
active_bills.pittsburgh_impact: Permanent solution to PRT's structural funding gap

active_bills.title: Transit for All PA — Senate companion (2)
active_bills.short_description: Companion bill in Senate for expanded transit funding.

active_bills.title: Middle Housing Zoning Reform
active_bills.short_description: Requires certain local governments to permit duplex, triplex, and quadplex housing in areas currently zoned single-family.
active_bills.status: Introduced February 2026; part of bipartisan housing package
active_bills.pittsburgh_impact: Would preempt exclusionary zoning in Pittsburgh suburbs and throughout Allegheny County

active_bills.title: ADU (Accessory Dwelling Unit) Reform
active_bills.short_description: Allows municipalities to enact 'reasonable' restrictions for ADUs rather than outright bans. Currently many PA municipalities ban ADUs.
active_bills.status: Introduced February 2026; part of bipartisan housing package
active_bills.pittsburgh_impact: Relevant to Pittsburgh's ongoing Housing Needs Assessment implementation (ADU legalization citywide)

active_bills.title: Parking Minimum Prohibition
active_bills.short_description: Prohibits mandatory minimum parking requirements, which drive up housing development costs.
active_bills.status: Introduced February 2026; part of bipartisan housing package
active_bills.pittsburgh_impact: Aligned with Pittsburgh's local parking reform efforts under Housing Needs Assessment

active_bills.title: Longtime Owner Occupant Program (LOOP)
active_bills.short_description: Signed into law July 2024. Allows Pittsburgh to exempt or defer property tax increases for homeowners who have lived in their homes for 10+ years in gentrifying neighborhoods.
active_bills.status: ENACTED — signed by Gov. Shapiro July 11, 2024. Pittsburgh City Council implementing in 2025.
active_bills.pittsburgh_impact: Direct relief for longtime Pittsburgh homeowners facing rapid assessment increases in neighborhoods like Lawrenceville, East Liberty, and Squirrel Hill.

active_bills.title: Reinvestment in Housing Act
active_bills.short_description: Prevents home improvements from triggering property reassessments for primary residences owned 5+ years. One-time benefit per property.
active_bills.status: Unanimously advanced by Senate Urban Affairs Committee November 2025

active_bills.title: School District Property Tax Elimination
active_bills.short_description: Would eliminate school district property taxes and replace with county and school district taxes. Amends Titles 53 (Municipalities) and 72 (Taxation).
active_bills.pittsburgh_impact: Major structural change for Pittsburgh school funding model; high opposition likely

active_bills.title: PA Safe Drinking Water Act — PFAS Testing
active_bills.short_description: Amends Pennsylvania Safe Drinking Water Act to require increased PFAS testing in public water systems via Environmental Quality Board.
active_bills.pittsburgh_impact: Affects PWSA (Pittsburgh Water and Sewer Authority) testing and compliance requirements

active_bills.title: DEP Permitting Reform
active_bills.short_description: Signed November 12, 2025 as part of FY 2025-26 budget. Expands SPEED program (third-party permit reviewers), creates 30-day shot clocks for air permits, deemed-approval if DEP misses deadlines. Notably excludes Allegheny County air permits (which fall under ACHD home rule).
active_bills.pittsburgh_impact: Expedites state permitting but Allegheny County retains its own air quality jurisdiction under ACHD. RGGI exit provisions remove greenhouse gas regulation framework.

active_bills.title: Source of Income Non-Discrimination
active_bills.short_description: Prohibits landlords from refusing to accept housing vouchers (Section 8, etc.) as income. PHRC annual fair housing report highlighted this as priority.
active_bills.pittsburgh_impact: Would expand housing access for voucher holders in Pittsburgh metro

active_bills.title: Digital Advertising on Transit Fleets
active_bills.short_description: Permits public transportation agencies to utilize digital advertising signage on their fleets. Passed Senate Transportation Committee September 2025.
active_bills.status: Reported out of Senate Transportation Committee with amendment
active_bills.pittsburgh_impact: PRT could utilize this as a revenue stream to offset operating deficits

key_actions.title: Housing Action Plan Directive
key_actions.description: Directed DCED to develop a comprehensive statewide housing plan. Led to the February 2026 Housing Action Plan release.
key_actions.pittsburgh_impact: Framework for statewide zoning reform, transit-oriented housing, and affordable housing investment

key_actions.title: Pennsylvania's First-Ever Housing Action Plan
key_actions.description: Nearly 30 strategic initiatives including: $1B critical infrastructure bond fund for housing construction, limits on rental application fees, eviction record sealing, transfer-on-death deeds, Deputy Secretary for Housing at DCED, Municipalities Planning Code modernization, lot rent increase caps in manufactured home communities.
key_actions.budget_ask: $1 billion in general obligation bonds for housing and infrastructure (FY 2026-27 budget)
key_actions.pittsburgh_impact: Downtown Pittsburgh real estate conversion (800 new housing units via downtown revitalization), ADU legalization, anti-displacement protections

key_actions.title: FY 2026-27 Executive Budget
key_items: New revenue via recreational marijuana legalization and skill game regulation
key_actions.pittsburgh_impact: Transit funding resolution critical for PRT long-term; housing investments; permitting reform

key_actions.title: Safeguarding Vaccine Access
key_actions.description: Directed PA DOH to create state safety net for vaccines following federal disruption. Less direct Pittsburgh impact.

key_actions.title: FY 2025-26 Budget Signed ($50.1B)
key_actions.description: 135-day late budget. Included environmental permitting reforms (Act 45), exit from RGGI, no new dedicated transit funding (Senate Republicans blocked). PRT forced to use $106.7M capital funds for operations.
key_actions.pittsburgh_impact: Failure to include transit funding = immediate PRT fiscal crisis. RGGI exit = loss of carbon pricing framework in PA.

key_actions.title: PennDOT Capital Funds Approval for PRT
key_actions.description: Shapiro administration approved PRT's request to use $106.7M in capital funds to cover operating deficit, avoiding service cuts and fare increases for 2 years.
key_actions.pittsburgh_impact: Preserved PRT service through FY 2025-26 and 2026-27 budget years pending permanent fix

key_actions.notes: Allegheny County Executive Sara Innamorato (D) — separate from state

county_eos.title: HOUSING (Housing Outcomes through Unified Strategies, Investment, and Governance) for All
Strengthening Worker Protections in Allegheny County

key_committees.name: Local Government Committee
key_committees.relevance: Handles municipal and county legislation, home rule charters, intergovernmental relations. Critical for any Pittsburgh/Allegheny County-specific bills.

key_committees.name: Local Government Committee
key_committees.relevance: Companion senate committee for municipal/county legislation. Sen. Amanda Cappelletti is Vice Chair (2025-26). Sen. Wayne Fontana (D-42, Allegheny) is a member.

key_committees.name: Urban Affairs Committee
key_committees.relevance: Handles housing, zoning, urban development, community development legislation.

key_committees.name: Urban Affairs & Housing Committee
key_committees.relevance: Key housing and zoning legislation. Sen. Picozzi (R) has introduced housing incentive bills. Sen. Fontana (D-42, Allegheny) is a member.
key_committees.notes: Chair Picozzi has proposed grants to localities that adopt pro-housing zoning and transit-oriented development policies.

key_committees.name: Transportation Committee
key_committees.relevance: Transit funding, road/bridge programs, PRT-related legislation. HB 1364 (Public Transportation Trust Fund) originated here.

key_committees.name: Transportation Committee
key_committees.relevance: Bottleneck for transit funding bills in 2025. SB 795/796 (Transit for All PA) awaiting action. HB 1364 (transit funding) passed House but stalled in Senate.

key_committees.name: Appropriations Committee
key_committees.relevance: Controls budget allocations including transit, housing, and environmental funding.

key_committees.name: Environmental Resources & Energy Committee
key_committees.relevance: PFAS, drinking water, DEP permitting reform, air quality legislation affecting PWSA and ACHD.

allegheny_delegation.notes: Allegheny County has 23 House seats and 5 Senate districts. All delegation members listed below. Democrats hold all Allegheny County Senate seats; House delegation is majority-Democratic.

state_senators.name: Lindsey Marie Williams
state_senators.notes: Minority member, Senate Transportation Committee. Co-sponsor of Transit for All PA (SB 795/796).

state_senators.name: Wayne D. Fontana
state_senators.notes: Active on housing and PWSA funding. Announced $26M PENNVEST grant to PWSA in Jan 2025.

state_senators.name: Jay Costa
state_senators.notes: Authored LOOP Act (Act 53 of 2024) for Pittsburgh property tax relief for longtime homeowners. Vocal advocate for PRT transit funding.

state_senators.name: Nick Pisciottano
state_senators.notes: Replaced James Brewster for 2025-2026 session. Co-sponsor of Transit for All PA.

state_representatives.name: Sara Innamorato
state_representatives.notes: Now Allegheny County Executive; seat may have changed

state_representatives.name: Summer Lee
state_representatives.notes: Now in Congress (PA-12); seat likely held by successor

state_representatives.name: John Inglis
state_representatives.notes: Sponsored HB 2185 (duplex/triplex zoning), HB 2186 (ADU allowances), and transit-oriented housing proposal

state_representatives.name: Anita Kulik
state_representatives.notes: Retiring 2026; 45th District open seat race underway

state_representatives.name: Austin Davis
state_representatives.notes: Now Lt. Governor; seat held by successor

regional_bodies.name: Southwestern Pennsylvania Commission (SPC)
regional_bodies.role: Federally-designated regional MPO for transportation and economic development. Administers $39B+ in state and federal transportation and economic development funding through 2050. Develops Long-Range Transportation Plan, Transportation Improvement Program (TIP), and other regional plans.
regional_bodies.coverage: 10-county region: Allegheny, Armstrong, Beaver, Butler, Fayette, Greene, Indiana, Lawrence, Washington, Westmoreland, plus City of Pittsburgh
initiatives: 2025-2028 Transportation Improvement Program (TIP) — recently completed public comment round (December 2025)
Long Range Transportation Plan (SmartMoves for a Changing Region)
2025 Regional Safety Action Plan (wrong-way crashes and safety initiatives)
Transportation Demand Management (TDM) Media Management Services RFP
Small business support and export assistance

meeting_schedule.full_commission: Quarterly — Feb 23, Apr 27, Jun 22, 2026 (2pm RPAC, 3pm Exec Committee, 4pm Full Commission)

current_initiatives: 2025-2028 Transportation Improvement Program (TIP) — recently completed public comment round (December 2025)
Long Range Transportation Plan (SmartMoves for a Changing Region)
2025 Regional Safety Action Plan (wrong-way crashes and safety initiatives)
Transportation Demand Management (TDM) Media Management Services RFP
Small business support and export assistance

regional_bodies.name: CONNECT (Congress of Neighboring Communities)
regional_bodies.role: Facilitates regional policy innovation. Municipalities share solutions to shared challenges. Acts as incubator for intergovernmental cooperation.
initiatives: Climate Action Plan (adopted unanimously by member municipalities in 2022, ongoing implementation)
Public Safety & Wellbeing Working Groups: LEAD (law enforcement assisted diversion), Future of Fire
Infrastructure & Resilience Working Groups: Climate Resilience, Solar, Composting
Regional wellbeing and sustainability collaboration (2025 annual recap: continued focus on these areas)
regional_bodies.meeting_schedule: Monthly member meetings (e.g., February 2026 featured Allegheny County Economic Development presentation). Schedule via connectgovs.org.
current_initiatives: Climate Action Plan (adopted unanimously by member municipalities in 2022, ongoing implementation)
Public Safety & Wellbeing Working Groups: LEAD (law enforcement assisted diversion), Future of Fire
Infrastructure & Resilience Working Groups: Climate Resilience, Solar, Composting
Regional wellbeing and sustainability collaboration (2025 annual recap: continued focus on these areas)

regional_bodies.name: Allegheny Conference on Community Development (ACCD)
regional_bodies.role: Leading public-private partnership for regional economic development. Convenes business, government, and civic leaders. Operates Pittsburgh Regional Alliance for business attraction. Not a government body but highly influential on state and county policy.
priorities: Continue AI/robotics, life sciences, and energy cluster development
Advance Downtown Pittsburgh revival (Market Square + 8th Street Block construction due April 2026)
Push for continued permitting and zoning streamlining
Modernize development processes to reduce timelines
accomplishments: 21 major business investments secured, 18,574 jobs created/retained, $16.1B in capital investment — strongest project year in over a decade
Downtown Pittsburgh revitalization: Arts Landing, Market Square redesign, 800 new housing units via real estate conversion
Supported PA permitting reform (Act 45 of 2025, SPEED program expansion)
Developed 'Build Now and For the Future' energy strategy for southwestern PA

regional_bodies.name: Allegheny County Sanitary Authority (ALCOSAN)
regional_bodies.role: Provides regional wastewater treatment for Allegheny County municipalities. Operates under a Modified Consent Decree with EPA and PA DEP requiring major CSO (combined sewer overflow) reduction.

board_meeting_schedule.frequency: Monthly, last Thursday of month, 4:30 PM
board_meeting_schedule.location: ALCOSAN Administration Building, 3300 Preble Avenue, Pittsburgh

consent_decree.program: Clean Water Plan — ongoing $3B+ capital program
consent_decree.plant_expansion: Expanding treatment capacity from 250 MGD to 480 MGD by end of 2026, then to 600 MGD by end of 2029/2030
major_projects: Ohio River Tunnel (ORT) — bids opened February 26, 2026
Allegheny River Tunnel (ART) — at ~30% design; Construction Management RFQ issued early 2026
North End Plant expansion (new outfall commissioned)
consent_decree.grow_program: Green Revitalization of Our Waterways — grants to municipalities for green infrastructure. 211 completed or active grants. ~$100M+ committed. Cycle 12/13 ongoing.

regional_bodies.name: Redevelopment Authority of Allegheny County (RAAC)
regional_bodies.role: Acquires and prepares local real estate for economic development. Manages financing from public sources. Facilitates reuse of vacant, tax-delinquent, or blighted properties in Allegheny County.
regional_bodies.meeting_schedule: All meetings start at 2:00 PM EST. Schedule available at meetings URL above.

regional_bodies.name: Allegheny County Health Department (ACHD)
regional_bodies.type: County Health Department with State-Delegated Authority
regional_bodies.role: Has home-rule authority for air quality permitting in Allegheny County (excluding state SPEED program scope). Issues Title V and Non-Title V operating permits, new source permits, modifications. Enforces county air quality regulations which are more stringent than state standards in some cases.
initiatives: Implementing increased fee structure for major industrial emitters
RACT III air permit determinations (NOx and VOC from major sources)
Responding to EPA delegation for federal plan emissions standards
Addressing PM2.5 nonattainment designation from DEP/EPA

public_comment_opportunities.subject: Air quality permits (specific facility not identified in source)
public_comment_opportunities.location: ACHD Air Quality, 836 Fulton Street, Pittsburgh, PA 15233

public_comment_opportunities.subject: Air quality permits (separate batch)
public_comment_opportunities.location: ACHD Air Quality, 836 Fulton Street, Pittsburgh, PA 15233
public_comment_opportunities.notes: Check ACHD public comment notices page for details and registration requirement (24hr advance registration, 3-min limit)

current_initiatives: Implementing increased fee structure for major industrial emitters
RACT III air permit determinations (NOx and VOC from major sources)
Responding to EPA delegation for federal plan emissions standards
Addressing PM2.5 nonattainment designation from DEP/EPA
regional_bodies.fee_update: Allegheny County Council voted November 2025 to significantly increase industrial air quality permit fees — U.S. Steel major source annual fee from $8,000 to $55,000; operating permit renewal from $4,000 to $20,000. First increase since 2021. Purpose: fund depleted ACHD Air Quality Program staffing.

data_sources.name: PA General Assembly Official Website
data_sources.description: Primary official source. Search bills by number, keyword, sponsor, or committee. Find committee schedules, session calendars, roll call votes, and member information. Operated by the PA Legislative Data Processing Center.
features: Bill search by number, keyword, sponsor, subject
Committee meeting schedules and archived votes
Session calendar and floor vote records
Member contact info and committee assignments
Printer's numbers (PN) to track bill versions
data_sources.notes: Direct robots.txt blocking of some automated access; manual browsing works.

data_sources.name: LegiScan
data_sources.description: Third-party bill tracking with PA-specific dashboard, full-text search, RSS feeds, email alerts, and downloadable session datasets. API available. Most-viewed and most-monitored bills ranked. Covers 2025-2026 Regular Session (2,967+ bills introduced as of mid-2025).

data_sources.name: OpenStates / Plural Policy
data_sources.description: Open-source bill tracking covering PA legislative sessions. Provides structured data on bills, votes, and legislators. API available at https://v3.openstates.org/. Current 2025-2026 session tracked.
features: RESTful API for bill and legislator data
Open data: bill status, sponsors, committee referrals

data_sources.name: PA Legislative Data Processing Center
data_sources.description: The official state body that maintains the PA General Assembly's digital records and powers palegis.us. Legislative data available via the official GA website.

data_sources.name: PA Capitol Tracker / PA Capital-Star
data_sources.description: Investigative journalism outlet covering PA legislature. Tracks key bills, budget negotiations, and Harrisburg politics with Western PA focus.
data_sources.notes: Not a formal bill tracker, but one of the best editorial sources for legislative context.

data_sources.name: Ballotpedia — Pennsylvania
data_sources.description: Covers PA legislative sessions, member profiles, committee assignments, and elections. Useful for historical context and candidate/incumbent information.
